title: Quiet hours for shared shelter

content:
Practical guidance for “Quiet hours for shared shelter” within “Shelter · Power · Navigation”. It supports life safety, official alerts, accountability, safe movement, shelter decisions, and controlled recovery, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Quiet hours for shared shelter” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Stop condition: Never enter moving floodwater, wildfire closure, smoke-obscured road, unstable slope, damaged structure, or officially restricted route. Apply this boundary to “Quiet hours for shared shelter” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Reduced fallback: If “Quiet hours for shared shelter” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use the safest verified shelter or route directed by the competent authority.

steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Quiet hours for shared shelter” in “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Confirm the reason for movement, official instruction, destination, primary route, fallback route, weather, daylight, and transport limits. Record the result under “Shelter · Power · Navigation” before continuing “Quiet hours for shared shelter”.

Mark hazards such as fire, smoke, floodwater, damaged bridges, unstable slopes, crowds, poor visibility, and restricted areas. Recheck this point before closing “Quiet hours for shared shelter” for “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Match pace, load, rest, assistance, and transport to children, older adults, disability, illness, animals, and the slowest safe member. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Quiet hours for shared shelter” for “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

Leave a departure time, route, destination, group list, check-in schedule, and missed-contact rule with a trusted person. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Quiet hours for shared shelter”.

Stop at planned checkpoints, compare real conditions with the map, and turn back or divert before the route becomes trapping or irreversible. Include the outcome of “Quiet hours for shared shelter” in the handover for “Shelter · Power · Navigation”.

This card is not authorization to back-feed a building, bypass protection, open an energized device, charge a damaged battery, or run fuel-burning equipment indoors.
